intent_topics (\n    intent_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n    topic_id  TEXT NOT NULL,\n    PRIMARY KEY (intent_id, topic_id),\n    FOREIGN KEY (intent_id) REFERENCES intents(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n    FOREIGN KEY (topic_id) REFERENCES topics(id) ON DELETE CASCADE\n);\n\n-- Feedback signals: implicit user behavior tracking for background analysis\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS feedback_signals (\n    signal_id    TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n    signal_type  TEXT NOT NULL,\n    session_id   TEXT NOT NULL,\n    result_id    TEXT,\n    topic_id     TEXT,\n    timestamp    INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    data         TEXT NOT NULL,  -- JSON: signal-specific data\n    surface_type TEXT,\n    processed    INTEGER DEFAULT 0 CHECK(processed IN (0, 1)),\n    processed_at INTEGER,\n    FOREIGN KEY (session_id) REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n    FOREIGN KEY (result_id) REFERENCES results(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,\n    FOREIGN KEY (topic_id) REFERENCES topics(id) ON DELETE SET NULL\n);\n\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_signals_session ON feedback_signals(session_id);\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_signals_type ON feedback_signals(signal_type);\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_signals_processed ON feedback_signals(processed);\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_signals_result ON feedback_signals(result_id);\n\n-- Per-stage latency capture for every dispatch (Latency Budget & Instrumentation).\n-- Keyed by the intent *thread* id (routed_intent.intent_id — the same id the\n-- fetch/synthesize/escalate strands and the results row use), NOT the\n-- intents.id the caller mints in create_intent(). router_ms is shared across\n-- every intent thread from the same utterance; escalate_ms is NULL for hot-path\n-- dispatches; stt_ms/first_render_ms are client-reported and NULL when the\n-- client never reports them. synthesize_first_token_ms is NULL until the\n-- synthesize strand streams (the non-streaming call_simple path can't measure\n-- first token separately) — see src/instrument/timings.py.\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS dispatch_timings (\n    intent_id                 TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n    router_ms                 INTEGER,\n    fetch_first_source_ms     INTEGER,\n    fetch_total_ms            INTEGER,\n    synthesize_first_token_ms INTEGER,\n    synthesize_total_ms       INTEGER,\n    escalate_ms               INTEGER,\n    sse_emit_ms               INTEGER,\n    stt_ms                    INTEGER,\n    first_render_ms           INTEGER,\n    created_at                INTEGER NOT NULL\n);\n\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_dispatch_timings_created ON dispatch_timings(created_at);\n\"\"\"\n\n# The set of dispatch_timings timing columns record_dispatch_timings() may set.\n# intent_id/created_at are handled explicitly (PK / insert-only timestamp).\nDISPATCH_TIMING_COLUMNS = (\n    \"router_ms\",\n    \"fetch_first_source_ms\",\n    \"fetch_total_ms\",\n    \"synthesize_first_token_ms\",\n    \"synthesize_total_ms\",\n    \"escalate_ms\",\n    \"sse_emit_ms\",\n    \"stt_ms\",\n    \"first_render_ms\",\n)\n\n\nclass SessionStore:\n    \"\"\"Session store with WAL mode for concurrent access.\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, db_path: Path):\n        self.db_path = db_path\n\n    async def initialize(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Initialize database with schema and WAL mode.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            # Enable WAL mode for concurrent reads during writes\n            await db.execute(\"PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL\")\n            await db.execute(\"PRAGMA synchronous=NORMAL\")\n\n            # Create schema\n            await db.executescript(SCHEMA_SQL)\n            await db.commit()\n\n            # Additive migrations: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS above creates new\n            # columns only for freshly-made DBs; an existing data/session.db\n            # (e.g. the live Hetzner box) keeps its old column set and must be\n            # ALTERed. Each statement is guarded by a PRAGMA table_info probe so\n            # it is idempotent and never re-runs once the column exists.\n            await self._migrate_additive_columns(db)\n\n    @staticmethod\n    async def _migrate_additive_columns(db: aiosqlite.Connection) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Idempotently add columns introduced after the initial schema.\"\"\"\n        async with db.execute(\"PRAGMA table_info(results)\") as cur:\n            result_cols = {row[1] for row in await cur.fetchall()}\n        if \"result_type\" not in result_cols:\n            await db.execute(\"ALTER TABLE results ADD COLUMN result_type TEXT\")\n        if \"card_fallback\" not in result_cols:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"ALTER TABLE results ADD COLUMN card_fallback INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0\"\n            )\n\n        async with db.execute(\"PRAGMA table_info(intents)\") as cur:\n            intent_cols = {row[1] for row in await cur.fetchall()}\n        if \"lookup_kind\" not in intent_cols:\n            await db.execute(\"ALTER TABLE intents ADD COLUMN lookup_kind TEXT\")\n\n        await db.commit()\n\n    async def close(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Close database connection pool.\"\"\"\n        # aiosqlite uses connection-per-context, so just ensure checkpoint\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\"PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)\")\n\n    # Session operations\n    async def create_session(self, session_id: str | None = None) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Create a new session and return its ID.\"\"\"\n        if not session_id:\n            session_id = str(uuid4())\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"INSERT OR IGNORE INTO sessions (id, created_at, last_active) VALUES (?, ?, ?)\",\n                (session_id, now, now)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n        return session_id\n\n    async def get_session(self, session_id: str) -> Optional[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get session by ID.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"SELECT * FROM sessions WHERE id = ?\", (session_id,)\n            ) as cursor:\n                row = await cursor.fetchone()\n                return dict(row) if row else None\n\n    async def update_session_activity(self, session_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Update session last_active timestamp.\"\"\"\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"UPDATE sessions SET last_active = ? WHERE id = ?\",\n                (now, session_id)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def set_primary_surface(self, session_id: str, surface_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Set the primary surface for a session.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"UPDATE sessions SET primary_surface_id = ? WHERE id = ?\",\n                (surface_id, session_id)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def delete_session(self, session_id: str) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Delete a session and every row tied to it. Returns a removal summary.\n\n        Used by test teardown (DELETE /api/v1/sessions/{id}) to guarantee a\n        session and all its topics/results/intents/utterances/surfaces/signal\n        rows are gone. Note: SQLite FK ``ON DELETE CASCADE`` is *not* enforced\n        here (``PRAGMA foreign_keys`` is never enabled), so child rows are\n        deleted explicitly, in dependency order, within one transaction.\n\n        The topics table references sessions with ``ON DELETE SET NULL``, so\n        topics are *not* cascade-removed even if FKs were on — deleting them\n        explicitly is required for a true clean teardown.\n\n        Returns ``{\"session_removed\": 0|1, \"topics_removed\": <int>}``.\n        \"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n\n            # Count what we're about to remove (before deleting).\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM topics WHERE session_id = ?\", (session_id,)\n            ) as cur:\n                topics_removed = (await cur.fetchone())[0]\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"SELECT 1 FROM sessions WHERE id = ?\", (session_id,)\n            ) as cur:\n                session_removed = 1 if await cur.fetchone() else 0\n\n            # Children first, parents last (matches FK dependency order).\n            await db.execute(\n                \"DELETE FROM feedback_signals WHERE session_id = ?\", (session_id,)\n            )\n            await db.execute(\n                \"DELETE FROM results WHERE session_id = ?\", (session_id,)\n            )\n            await db.execute(\n                \"DELETE FROM intent_topics WHERE intent_id IN \"\n                \"(SELECT id FROM intents WHERE session_id = ?)\",\n                (session_id,),\n            )\n            # dispatch_timings is keyed by the intent *thread* id\n            # (routed_intent.intent_id), which differs from intents.id for the\n            # current router path (see the table comment in SCHEMA_SQL), so this\n            # is best-effort: it catches rows keyed by the store intent id, while\n            # thread-keyd rows survive until the thread id is known to the caller.\n            await db.execute(\n                \"DELETE FROM dispatch_timings WHERE intent_id IN \"\n                \"(SELECT id FROM intents WHERE session_id = ?)\",\n                (session_id,),\n            )\n            await db.execute(\n                \"DELETE FROM intents WHERE session_id = ?\", (session_id,)\n            )\n            await db.execute(\n                \"DELETE FROM utterances WHERE session_id = ?\", (session_id,)\n            )\n            await db.execute(\n                \"DELETE FROM topic_context_cache WHERE topic_id IN \"\n                \"(SELECT id FROM topics WHERE session_id = ?)\",\n                (session_id,),\n            )\n            await db.execute(\n                \"DELETE FROM topics WHERE session_id = ?\", (session_id,)\n            )\n            await db.execute(\n                \"DELETE FROM surfaces WHERE session_id = ?\", (session_id,)\n            )\n            await db.execute(\n                \"DELETE FROM sessions WHERE id = ?\", (session_id,)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n        return {\"session_removed\": session_removed, \"topics_removed\": topics_removed}\n\n    # Surface operations\n    async def register_surface(\n        self,\n        session_id: str,\n        surface_type: str,\n        always_available: bool = False,\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Register a new surface for a session.\"\"\"\n        surface_id = str(uuid4())\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"\"\"INSERT INTO surfaces\n                   (id, session_id, type, state, always_available, last_seen)\n                   VALUES (?, ?, ?, 'active', ?, ?)\"\"\",\n                (surface_id, session_id, surface_type, 1 if always_available else 0, now)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n        return surface_id\n\n    async def update_surface_heartbeat(self, surface_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Update surface last_seen timestamp.\"\"\"\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"UPDATE surfaces SET last_seen = ?, state = 'active' WHERE id = ?\",\n                (now, surface_id)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def mark_surface_disconnected(self, surface_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Mark a surface as disconnected.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"UPDATE surfaces SET state = 'disconnected' WHERE id = ?\",\n                (surface_id,)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def get_active_surfaces(self, session_id: str) -> list[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get all active surfaces for a session, ordered by last_seen.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT * FROM surfaces\n                   WHERE session_id = ? AND state IN ('active', 'idle')\n                   ORDER BY last_seen DESC\"\"\",\n                (session_id,)\n            ) as cursor:\n                rows = await cursor.fetchall()\n                return [dict(row) for row in rows]\n\n    async def get_fallback_surface(self) -> Optional[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get the always-available fallback surface (Telegram).\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT * FROM surfaces\n                   WHERE always_available = 1 AND state != 'disconnected'\n                   LIMIT 1\"\"\"\n            ) as cursor:\n                row = await cursor.fetchone()\n                return dict(row) if row else None\n\n    # Utterance operations\n    async def create_utterance(self, session_id: str, raw_text: str, utterance_id: str | None = None) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Create a new utterance and return its ID.\"\"\"\n        utterance_id = utterance_id or str(uuid4())\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"INSERT INTO utterances (id, session_id, raw_text, created_at) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)\",\n                (utterance_id, session_id, raw_text, now)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n        return utterance_id\n\n    # Intent operations\n    async def create_intent(\n        self,\n        utterance_id: str,\n        session_id: str,\n        project_slug: str | None,\n        intent_type: str,\n        bead_ref: str | None = None,\n        lookup_kind: str | None = None,\n        topic_id: str | None = None,\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Create a new intent and return its ID.\"\"\"\n        intent_id = str(uuid4())\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"\"\"INSERT INTO intents\n                   (id, utterance_id, session_id, project_slug, intent_type, lookup_kind, bead_ref, topic_id, created_at)\n                   VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\"\"\",\n                (intent_id, utterance_id, session_id, project_slug, intent_type, lookup_kind, bead_ref, topic_id, now)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n        return intent_id\n\n    async def update_intent_status(\n        self,\n        intent_id: str,\n        status: str,\n        resolved_at: int | None = None,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Update intent status.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            if resolved_at is None and status == \"resolved\":\n                resolved_at = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n            await db.execute(\n                \"UPDATE intents SET status = ?, resolved_at = ? WHERE id = ?\",\n                (status, resolved_at, intent_id)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def get_pending_intents(self, session_id: str) -> list[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get all pending intents for a session.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT * FROM intents\n                   WHERE session_id = ? AND status IN ('pending', 'dispatched')\n                   ORDER BY created_at DESC\"\"\",\n                (session_id,)\n            ) as cursor:\n                rows = await cursor.fetchall()\n                return [dict(row) for row in rows]\n\n    async def get_intent_by_bead_ref(self, bead_ref: str) -> Optional[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get intent by bead reference where status is tracked.\n\n        Returns intent row WHERE bead_ref == <bead_ref> AND status IN\n        ('pending', 'dispatched', 'stuck'), or None if no match.\n        Used by the bead watcher to resolve closed beads to their\n        original intent context.\n        \"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT * FROM intents\n                   WHERE bead_ref = ? AND status IN ('pending', 'dispatched', 'stuck')\n                   LIMIT 1\"\"\",\n                (bead_ref,)\n            ) as cursor:\n                row = await cursor.fetchone()\n                return dict(row) if row else None\n\n    async def get_intent(self, intent_id: str) -> Optional[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get intent by ID.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT * FROM intents WHERE id = ? LIMIT 1\"\"\",\n                (intent_id,)\n            ) as cursor:\n                row = await cursor.fetchone()\n                return dict(row) if row else None\n\n    # Result operations\n    async def create_result(\n        self,\n        intent_id: str,\n        topic_id: str,\n        session_id: str,\n        summary: str,\n        data: dict,\n        urgency: str = \"normal\",\n        previous_result_id: str | None = None,\n        diff_summary: str | None = None,\n        diff_data: dict | None = None,\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Create a new result and return its ID.\"\"\"\n        import json\n        result_id = str(uuid4())\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"\"\"INSERT INTO results\n                   (id, intent_id, topic_id, session_id, summary, data, urgency, created_at, surfaced_at,\n                    previous_result_id, diff_summary, diff_data)\n                   VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\"\"\",\n                (\n                    result_id, intent_id, topic_id, session_id, summary, json.dumps(data),\n                    urgency, now, now, previous_result_id, diff_summary,\n                    json.dumps(diff_data) if diff_data else None\n                )\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n        return result_id\n\n    async def get_unsurfed_results(self, session_id: str) -> list[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get results that haven't been surfaced to the user yet.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT * FROM results\n                   WHERE session_id = ? AND surfaced_at IS NULL\n                   ORDER BY created_at ASC\"\"\",\n                (session_id,)\n            ) as cursor:\n                rows = await cursor.fetchall()\n                return [dict(row) for row in rows]\n\n    async def mark_results_surfed(self, session_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Mark all unsurfed results as surfaced.\"\"\"\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"UPDATE results SET surfaced_at = ? WHERE session_id = ? AND surfaced_at IS NULL\",\n                (now, session_id)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def mark_results_surfed_by_ids(self, session_id: str, result_ids: list[str]) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Mark specific results as surfaced.\"\"\"\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            for result_id in result_ids:\n                await db.execute(\n                    \"UPDATE results SET surfaced_at = ? WHERE id = ? AND session_id = ?\",\n                    (now, result_id, session_id)\n                )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def get_results_for_intent(self, intent_id: str) -> list[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get all results for a specific intent.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT * FROM results\n                   WHERE intent_id = ?\n                   ORDER BY created_at DESC\"\"\",\n                (intent_id,)\n            ) as cursor:\n                rows = await cursor.fetchall()\n                return [dict(row) for row in rows]\n\n    async def get_all_results(self) -> list[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get all results in the database.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT * FROM results ORDER BY created_at DESC\"\"\"\n            ) as cursor:\n                rows = await cursor.fetchall()\n                return [dict(row) for row in rows]\n\n    # Topic operations\n    async def create_topic(\n        self,\n        label: str,\n        topic_type: str = \"adhoc\",\n        project_slugs: list[str] | None = None,\n        scope: str = \"session\",\n        session_id: str | None = None,\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Create a new topic and return its ID.\"\"\"\n        import json\n        topic_id = str(uuid4())\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"\"\"INSERT INTO topics\n                   (id, label, type, project_slugs, scope, session_id, created_at, last_active)\n                   VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\"\"\",\n                (topic_id, label, topic_type, json.dumps(project_slugs or []), scope, session_id, now, now)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n        return topic_id\n\n    async def find_or_create_topic(\n        self,\n        label: str,\n        session_id: str,\n        topic_type: str = \"adhoc\",\n        project_slugs: list[str] | None = None,\n    ) -> tuple[str, bool]:\n        \"\"\"Find existing topic by label and session, or create new. Returns (topic_id, created).\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            # Try to find existing topic in session scope\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT id FROM topics\n                   WHERE label = ? AND scope = 'session' AND session_id = ? AND archived_at IS NULL\n                   LIMIT 1\"\"\",\n                (label, session_id)\n            ) as cursor:\n                row = await cursor.fetchone()\n                if row:\n                    return row[0], False\n\n        # Create new topic\n        topic_id = await self.create_topic(label, topic_type, project_slugs, \"session\", session_id)\n        return topic_id, True\n\n    async def update_topic_activity(self, topic_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Update topic last_active timestamp.\"\"\"\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"UPDATE topics SET last_active = ? WHERE id = ?\",\n                (now, topic_id)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def get_active_topics(self, session_id: str) -> list[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get active topics for a session, ordered by last_active.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT t.*,\n                          (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM results r WHERE r.topic_id = t.id) as result_count\n                   FROM topics t\n                   WHERE (t.session_id = ? OR t.scope IN ('cross-session', 'global'))\n                     AND t.archived_at IS NULL\n                   ORDER BY t.last_active DESC\"\"\",\n                (session_id,)\n            ) as cursor:\n                rows = await cursor.fetchall()\n                return [dict(row) for row in rows]\n\n    async def get_latest_result_for_topic(self, topic_id: str) -> Optional[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get the most recent result for a topic.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT * FROM results\n                   WHERE topic_id = ?\n                   ORDER BY created_at DESC\n                   LIMIT 1\"\"\",\n                (topic_id,)\n            ) as cursor:\n                row = await cursor.fetchone()\n                return dict(row) if row else None\n\n    async def link_intent_to_topic(self, intent_id: str, topic_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Link an intent to a topic (many-to-many).\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"INSERT OR IGNORE INTO intent_topics (intent_id, topic_id) VALUES (?, ?)\",\n                (intent_id, topic_id)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    # Workload summary for reconnect\n    async def get_workload_summary(self, session_id: str) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Get summary of what happened while surface was disconnected.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            # Get pending intents\n            pending_count = (await db.execute_fetchall(\n                \"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM intents WHERE session_id = ? AND status IN ('pending', 'dispatched')\",\n                (session_id,)\n            ))[0][0]\n\n            # Get new results (unsurfaced results)\n            new_results = (await db.execute_fetchall(\n                \"\"\"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM results\n                   WHERE session_id = ? AND surfaced_at IS NULL\"\"\",\n                (session_id,)\n            ))[0][0]\n\n            # Get exceptions (HUMAN beads or high urgency items)\n            exceptions = (await db.execute_fetchall(\n                \"\"\"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM results\n                   WHERE session_id = ? AND urgency IN ('critical', 'high') AND acked_at IS NULL\"\"\",\n                (session_id,)\n            ))[0][0]\n\n            return {\n                \"pending_intents\": pending_count,\n                \"new_results\": new_results,\n                \"unresolved_exceptions\": exceptions,\n            }\n\n    # Topic context cache operations\n    async def set_topic_context(\n        self,\n        topic_id: str,\n        context_data: dict,\n        ttl_seconds: int = 600,  # Default 10 minutes\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Cache pre-fetched context for a topic.\"\"\"\n        import json\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        expires_at = now + ttl_seconds\n\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"\"\"INSERT OR REPLACE INTO topic_context_cache\n                   (topic_id, context_data, fetched_at, expires_at)\n                   VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)\"\"\",\n                (topic_id, json.dumps(context_data), now, expires_at)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def get_topic_context(self, topic_id: str) -> Optional[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get cached context for a topic if still valid.\"\"\"\n        import json\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT context_data, expires_at FROM topic_context_cache\n                   WHERE topic_id = ? AND expires_at > ?\"\"\",\n                (topic_id, now)\n            ) as cursor:\n                row = await cursor.fetchone()\n                if row:\n                    return {\n                        \"context\": json.loads(row[\"context_data\"]),\n                        \"expires_at\": row[\"expires_at\"],\n                    }\n        return None\n\n    async def invalidate_topic_context(self, topic_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Invalidate cached context for a topic.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"DELETE FROM topic_context_cache WHERE topic_id = ?\",\n                (topic_id,)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def cleanup_expired_context(self) -> int:\n        \"\"\"Remove expired context cache entries. Returns count of deleted entries.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            cursor = await db.execute(\n                \"DELETE FROM topic_context_cache WHERE expires_at <= ?\",\n                (int(datetime.now().timestamp()),)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n            return cursor.rowcount\n\n    async def get_active_topic_ids(self) -> list[str]:\n        \"\"\"Get IDs of all active topics (recently active, not archived).\"\"\"\n        # Active topics are those with activity in last hour\n        one_hour_ago = int(datetime.now().timestamp()) - 3600\n\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT id FROM topics\n                   WHERE last_active > ? AND archived_at IS NULL\"\"\",\n                (one_hour_ago,)\n            ) as cursor:\n                rows = await cursor.fetchall()\n                return [row[0] for row in rows]\n\n    # Feedback signal operations\n    async def create_feedback_signal(\n        self,\n        signal_id: str,\n        signal_type: str,\n        session_id: str,\n        result_id: str | None = None,\n        topic_id: str | None = None,\n        timestamp: int | None = None,\n        data: dict | None = None,\n        surface_type: str | None = None,\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Create a new feedback signal and return its ID.\"\"\"\n        import json\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"\"\"INSERT INTO feedback_signals\n                   (signal_id, signal_type, session_id, result_id, topic_id, timestamp, data, surface_type)\n                   VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\"\"\",\n                (\n                    signal_id, signal_type, session_id, result_id,\n                    topic_id, timestamp or now, json.dumps(data or {}),\n                    surface_type\n                )\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n        return signal_id\n\n    async def get_unprocessed_signals(\n        self,\n        signal_type: str | None = None,\n        limit: int = 100,\n    ) -> list[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get unprocessed feedback signals for background analysis.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            query = \"\"\"SELECT * FROM feedback_signals WHERE processed = 0\"\"\"\n            params = []\n\n            if signal_type:\n                query += \" AND signal_type = ?\"\n                params.append(signal_type)\n\n            query += \" ORDER BY timestamp ASC LIMIT ?\"\n            params.append(limit)\n\n            async with db.execute(query, params) as cursor:\n                rows = await cursor.fetchall()\n                return [dict(row) for row in rows]\n\n    async def mark_signals_processed(\n        self,\n        signal_ids: list[str],\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Mark feedback signals as processed.\"\"\"\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            for signal_id in signal_ids:\n                await db.execute(\n                    \"UPDATE feedback_signals SET processed = 1, processed_at = ? WHERE signal_id = ?\",\n                    (now, signal_id)\n                )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    # Dispatch timing operations (Latency Budget & Instrumentation)\n    async def record_dispatch_timings(\n        self,\n        intent_id: str,\n        **fields: int | None,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Persist per-stage timings for one dispatch intent thread.\n\n        Upserts the row so the same intent_id can be written incrementally:\n        process_intent() records the server-side stages it measures, the\n        dispatch caller records sse_emit_ms after the SSE broadcast, and the\n        /api/v1/timings endpoint records client-reported stt_ms/first_render_ms.\n        Only columns passed with a non-NULL value are touched, so a later\n        partial write never clobbers a stage an earlier write already set.\n        ``created_at`` is stamped once on insert and never overwritten.\n        \"\"\"\n        cols = {\n            k: v for k, v in fields.items()\n            if k in DISPATCH_TIMING_COLUMNS and v is not None\n        }\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            # Ensure the row exists (created_at set once, on first write).\n            await db.execute(\n                \"INSERT OR IGNORE INTO dispatch_timings (intent_id, created_at) VALUES (?, ?)\",\n                (intent_id, now),\n            )\n            if cols:\n                set_clause = \", \".join(f\"{c} = ?\" for c in cols)\n                params = [*cols.values(), intent_id]\n                await db.execute(\n                    f\"UPDATE dispatch_timings SET {set_clause} WHERE intent_id = ?\",\n                    params,\n                )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def get_dispatch_timings(self, intent_id: str) -> Optional[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get the dispatch_timings row for one intent thread, or None.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"SELECT * FROM dispatch_timings WHERE intent_id = ?\",\n                (intent_id,),\n            ) as cursor:\n                row = await cursor.fetchone()\n                return dict(row) if row else None\n\n    async def get_latency_percentiles(self, since: int | None = None) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Aggregate p50/p95 per stage across dispatch_timings rows.\n\n        Returns ``{stage: {\"p50\": ms, \"p95\": ms, \"count\": n}}`` for every stage\n        that has at least one non-NULL value. NULLs are skipped per stage (so a\n        stage not yet captured for any dispatch simply doesn't appear). Pass\n        ``since`` (a unix timestamp) to window to recent dispatches only — the\n        latency-baseline bead consumes the un-windowed call to fill the plan's\n        global Measured p50/p95 columns.\n\n        Percentiles are nearest-rank (ceil(q/100 * n)), computed in Python\n        because SQLite has no built-in percentile.\n        \"\"\"\n        from ..instrument.timings import percentiles\n\n        result: dict[str, dict] = {}\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            for stage in DISPATCH_TIMING_COLUMNS:\n                if since is not None:\n                    query = (\n                        f\"SELECT {stage} FROM dispatch_timings \"\n                        f\"WHERE {stage} IS NOT NULL AND created_at >= ? \"\n                        f\"ORDER BY {stage}\"\n                    )\n                    params: tuple = (since,)\n                else:\n                    query = (\n                        f\"SELECT {stage} FROM dispatch_timings \"\n                        f\"WHERE {stage} IS NOT NULL ORDER BY {stage}\"\n                    )\n                    params = ()\n                async with db.execute(query, params) as cursor:\n                    values = [row[0] for row in await cursor.fetchall()]\n                if not values:\n                    continue\n                pct = percentiles(values, qs=(50, 95))\n                result[stage] = {\n                    \"p50\": pct[50],\n                    \"p95\": pct[95],\n                    \"count\": len(values),\n                }\n        return result\n\n    async def get_session_feedback_summary(\n        self,\n        session_id: str,\n        since: int | None = None,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Get a summary of feedback signals for a session.\"\"\"\n        one_hour_ago = int(datetime.now().timestamp()) - 3600\n\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n\n            # Get signal counts by type\n            since_clause = since or one_hour_ago\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT signal_type, COUNT(*) as count\n                   FROM feedback_signals\n                   WHERE session_id = ? AND timestamp >= ?\n                   GROUP BY signal_type\"\"\",\n                (session_id, since_clause)\n            ) as cursor:\n                type_counts = {row[\"signal_type\"]: row[\"count\"] for row in await cursor.fetchall()}\n\n            # Get average ack delay\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT AVG(CAST(data->>'ack_delay_seconds' AS INTEGER)) as avg_delay\n                   FROM feedback_signals\n                   WHERE session_id = ? AND signal_type = 'ack_speed' AND timestamp >= ?\"\"\",\n                (session_id, since_clause)\n            ) as cursor:\n                row = await cursor.fetchone()\n                avg_ack_delay = row[\"avg_delay\"] if row and row[\"avg_delay\"] else 0\n\n            return {\n                \"signal_counts\": type_counts,\n                \"avg_ack_delay_seconds\": avg_ack_delay,\n            }\n\n    async def create_result_with_diff(\n        self,\n        intent_id: str,\n        topic_id: str,\n        session_id: str,\n        summary: str,\n        data: dict,\n        urgency: str = \"normal\",\n    ) -> tuple[str, bool]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Create a new result with automatic diff computation.\n\n        Returns (result_id, has_diff).\n\n        Convenience method that automatically computes the diff against\n        the previous result for the same topic (if one exists).\n        \"\"\"\n        from ..diff.engine import get_diff_engine\n\n        # Get previous result for this topic\n        previous_result = await self.get_latest_result_for_topic(topic_id)\n\n        # Compute diff if we have a previous result\n        diff_summary = None\n        diff_data = None\n        has_diff = False\n\n        if previous_result:\n            diff_engine = get_diff_engine()\n            diff_result = await diff_engine.compute_diff(\n                topic_id=topic_id,\n                previous_result=previous_result,\n                current_result={\"data\": data},\n            )\n\n            if diff_result.has_changes:\n                has_diff = True\n                diff_summary = diff_result.change_summary\n                diff_data = {\n                    \"fields\": [\n                        {\n                            \"field_name\": f.field_name,\n                            \"old_value\": f.old_value,\n                            \"new_value\": f.new_value,\n                            \"change_type\": f.change_type,\n                        }\n                        for f in diff_result.fields\n                    ],\n                    \"summary\": diff_result.summary,\n                }\n\n        # Create the result with diff data\n        result_id = await self.create_result(\n            intent_id=intent_id,\n            topic_id=topic_id,\n            session_id=session_id,\n            summary=summary,\n            data=data,\n            urgency=urgency,\n            previous_result_id=previous_result[\"id\"] if previous_result else None,\n            diff_summary=diff_summary,\n            diff_data=diff_data,\n        )\n\n        return result_id, has_diff\n\n\n# Global session store instance\n_store: SessionStore | None = None\n\n\ndef get_store(db_path: Path | None = None) -> SessionStore:\n    \"\"\"Get or create the global session store instance.\n\n    When db_path is not given, resolves in this order:\n      1. ADC_DB_PATH env var (used by tests to isolate from production data)\n      2. DEFAULT_DB_PATH (data/session.db)\n    \"\"\"\n    global _store\n    if _store is None:\n        if db_path is None:\n            env_path = os.environ.get(\"ADC_DB_PATH\")\n            db_path = Path(env_path) if env_path else DEFAULT_DB_PATH\n        _store = SessionStore(db_path)\n    return _store\n","numLines":1108,"startLine":1,"totalLines":1108}}}

HWM advances to the newest close seen this tick.\n        assert w._close_highwater == pytest.approx(\n            BeadWatcher._parse_close_epoch(\"2026-07-22T12:47:22Z\")\n        )\n\n    async def test_bead_at_or_below_hwm_not_emitted(self, store, router):\n        at = _bead(bead_id=\"adc-at\", closed_at=\"2026-07-22T12:47:22Z\")\n        below = _bead(bead_id=\"adc-below\", closed_at=\"2026-07-22T12:00:00Z\")\n        hw = BeadWatcher._parse_close_epoch(\"2026-07-22T12:47:22Z\")\n        w = _watcher_with_records(store, router, [at, below], highwater=hw)\n        assert await w._poll_closed_beads() == []  # strictly-newer only; == excluded\n\n    async def test_same_records_second_tick_emits_nothing(self, store, router):\n        rec = _bead(bead_id=\"adc-x\", closed_at=\"2026-07-22T12:47:22Z\")\n        w = _watcher_with_records(store, router, [rec])\n        assert await w._poll_closed_beads() == []  # Direct open() of a path containing '.beads/'\n2. Path operations on paths containing '.beads/'\n3. Old BEADS_JSONL or issues.jsonl constant usage\n\nThe test scans src/ (especially src/watcher/ and src/) for these patterns\nand fails if any are found. This ensures the CLI-only contract is maintained\neven as the codebase evolves.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport re\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nimport pytest\n\n\n# Grep patterns that indicate direct .beads/ file access\nVIOLATION_PATTERNS = [\n    # Direct open() of .beads/ paths\n    r'open\\([^)]*\\.beads/',\n    # Direct Path() operations on .beads/ that READ files (not .exists())\n    r'Path\\([^)]*\\.beads/.*\\)\\s*\\.read',\n    # Old constant names that should not exist\n    r'BEADS_JSONL',\n    # Direct reading of .beads/ files\n    r'\\.read_text\\([^)]*\\.beads/',\n    r'\\.read_bytes\\([^)]*\\.beads/',\n    # aiofiles/asyncio file operations on .beads/\n    r'aiofiles\\.open\\([^)]*\\.beads/',\n    r'asyncio.*open\\([^)]*\\.beads/',\n]\n\n# Additional patterns to check - but NOT shell existence checks or comments\n# These patterns are ok in certain contexts (shell checks, comments)\nCONTEXTUAL_PATTERNS = {\n    # issues.jsonl is OK only in shell checks [ -f file ] and comments\n    r'\\[ -f .*\\.beads/issues\\.jsonl': 'shell-existence-check',\n}\n\n# Files/directories that are exempt from this check (e.g., test fixtures, comments)\nEXEMPT_PATHS = [\n    # This test file itself\n    \"test_bead_watcher_grep_guard.py\",\n    # Comments/documentation are fine\n]\n\n# Pattern strings that are exempt (e.g., in comments explaining the invariant)\nEXEMPT_PATTERNS = [\n    # Allow in comments (lines starting with #)\n    r'^#.*\\.beads/',\n    # Allow in docstrings (triple quotes on line)\n    r'^[^#]*\"\"\".*\\.beads/',\n]\n\n\ndef is_exempt(line: str) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Check if a line is exempt from violation detection.\"\"\"\n    for exempt_pattern in EXEMPT_PATTERNS:\n        if re.search(exempt_pattern, line):\n            return True\n    # Shell existence checks are OK\n    if '[ -f ' in line and '.beads/' in line:\n        return True\n    # Field annotations and type comments are OK\n    if ': bool' in line or '# ' in line:\n        return True\n    return False\n\n\ndef find_violations() -> list[tuple[str, int, str, str]]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Scan src/ for CLI-only invariant violations.\n\n    Returns:\n        List of (file_path, line_number, line_text, pattern) tuples\n    \"\"\"\n    src_dir = Path(__file__).parent.parent / \"src\"\n    violations = []\n\n    for py_file in src_dir.rglob(\"*.py\"):\n        # Skip exempt paths\n        if any(exempt in str(py_file) for exempt in EXEMPT_PATHS):\n            continue\n\n        try:\n            with open(py_file, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:\n                for line_num, line in enumerate(f, start=1):\n                    # Skip exempt lines (comments, etc.)\n                    if is_exempt(line):\n                        continue\n\n                    # Check each violation pattern\n                    for pattern in VIOLATION_PATTERNS:\n                        if re.search(pattern, line):\n                            violations.append((\n                                str(py_file.relative_to(src_dir.parent)),\n                                line_num,\n                                line.strip(),\n                                pattern,\n                            ))\n                            # Don't report multiple patterns for same line\n                            break\n        except Exception:\n            # If we can't read the file, skip it (e.g., binary, permissions)\n            continue\n\n    return violations\n\n\n@pytest.mark.parametrize(\n    \"pattern\",\n    VIOLATION_PATTERNS,\n    ids=lambda p: p[:30] + \"...\" if len(p) > 30 else p,\n)\ndef test_no_direct_beads_file_access(pattern):\n    \"\"\"\n    Test that no code path under src/ directly opens .beads/ files.\n\n    This enforces the CLI-only invariant from plan §10 Bead Watcher:\n    the watcher polls `bf list --status closed` and never reads the\n    bf workspace's private store files directly (both are documented\n    corruption/staleness footguns in this workspace).\n\n    If this test fails, it means someone has reintroduced direct .beads/\n    file access, which violates the CLI-only contract and risks data\n    corruption or staleness bugs.\n    \"\"\"\n    violations = find_violations()\n\n    # Filter violations for this specific pattern\n    pattern_violations = [v for v in violations if v[3] == pattern]\n\n    if pattern_violations:\n        # Build a helpful error message\n        msg = f\"Found {len(pattern_violations)} violation(s) of pattern: {pattern}\\n\\n\"\n        for file_path, line_num, line_text, _ in pattern_violations:\n            msg += f\"  {file_path}:{line_num}: {line_text}\\n\"\n        msg += \"\\nThis violates the CLI-only invariant (plan §10 Bead Watcher).\\n\"\n        msg += \"The bead watcher must use `bf list --status closed` instead.\\n\"\n        pytest.fail(msg)\n\n\ndef test_no_direct_beads_file_access_combined():\n    \"\"\"\n    Combined test: check all violation patterns at once for faster feedback.\n\n    This is the main test that runs in CI - it checks all patterns in one pass\n    and provides a comprehensive report of any violations.\n    \"\"\"\n    violations = find_violations()\n\n    if violations:\n        # Group violations by file for clearer output\n        by_file: dict[str, list[tuple[int, str, str]]] = {}\n        for file_path, line_num, line_text, pattern in violations:\n            if file_path not in by_file:\n                by_file[file_path] = []\n            by_file[file_path].append((line_num, line_text, pattern))\n\n        # Build helpful error message\n        msg = f\"Found {len(violations)} CLI-only invariant violation(s) across {len(by_file)} file(s):\\n\\n\"\n        for file_path, file_violations in sorted(by_file.items()):\n            msg += f\"{file_path}:\\n\"\n            for line_num, line_text, pattern in file_violations:\n                msg += f\"  Line {line_num}: {line_text}\\n\"\n                msg += f\"    Pattern: {pattern}\\n\"\n            msg += \"\\n\"\n\n        msg += \"This violates the CLI-only invariant (plan §10 Bead Watcher).\\n\"\n        msg += \"The bead watcher must use `bf list --status closed` via subprocess.\\n\"\n        msg += \"Direct access to .beads/ files risks corruption/staleness bugs.\\n\"\n        pytest.fail(msg)\n\n\ndef test_violation_detection_works():\n    \"\"\"\n    Meta-test: verify that the violation detector actually catches violations.\n\n    This test intentionally introduces a violation in a temp file to prove\n    that the grep-guard machinery works. If this test fails, it means the\n    violation detection logic is broken.\n    \"\"\"\n    import tempfile\n    import shutil\n\n    # Create a temp directory structure\n    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:\n        tmpdir_path = Path(tmpdir)\n        src_dir = tmpdir_path / \"src\"\n        src_dir.mkdir()\n\n        # Create a test file with intentional violations\n        test_file = src_dir / \"test_violations.py\"\n        test_file.write_text('''\n# This file has intentional violations for testing\n\n# Violation 1: direct open()\ndata = open(\"/path/to/.beads/issues.jsonl\")\n\n# Violation 2: Path operations with read()\np = Path(\".beads/data.db\").read_text()\n\n# Violation 3: old constant name\nBEADS_JSONL = \"/path/to/issues.jsonl\"\n\n# This comment should NOT trigger (it's a comment)\n# data = open(\".beads/file\")\n''')\n\n        # Scan for violations in the temp directory\n        violations = []\n        for py_file in src_dir.rglob(\"*.py\"):\n            with open(py_file, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:\n                for line_num, line in enumerate(f, start=1):\n                    if is_exempt(line):\n                        continue\n                    for pattern in VIOLATION_PATTERNS:\n                        if re.search(pattern, line):\n                            violations.append((\n                                str(py_file),\n                                line_num,\n                                line.strip(),\n                                pattern,\n                            ))\n                            break\n\n        # We should have caught exactly 3 violations (open(), Path().read, BEADS_JSONL)\n        # Note: Path() alone is not a violation - only when it reads files\n        assert len(violations) == 3, f\"Expected 3 violations, found {len(violations)}: {violations}\"\n\n        # Verify we caught the right lines\n        violation_lines = {v[1] for v in violations}\n        assert 5 in violation_lines  # open()\n        assert 8 in violation_lines  # Path().read (we need to add .read to trigger violation)\n        assert 11 in violation_lines  # BEADS_JSONL\n\n        # The comment on line 14 should NOT be a violation\n        assert 14 not in violation_lines\n\n\ndef test_comment_lines_are_exempt():\n    \"\"\"Test that comment lines explaining the invariant are not flagged.\"\"\"\n    # A line with .beads/ in a comment should be exempt\n    comment_line = \"# This code uses bf CLI instead of reading .beads/issues.jsonl directly\"\n    assert is_exempt(comment_line)\n\n    # A line with .beads/ in a docstring should be exempt\n    docstring_line = '\"\"\"The watcher polls bf instead of reading .beads/ store.\"\"\"'\n    assert is_exempt(docstring_line)\n\n    # Actual code should NOT be exempt\n    code_line = 'data = open(\".beads/file.txt\")'\n    assert not is_exempt(code_line)\n","numLines":261,"startLine":1,"totalLines":261}}}

Create an intent with bead_ref pointing at a test bead\n    2. Simulate bf CLI returning the closed bead\n    3. Run one watcher tick\n    4. Assert:\n       - Results row written to DB\n       - Intent status updated to resolved\n       - SSE result_created event fired on active surface\n    \"\"\"\n    # Step 1: Set up session, surface, utterance, topic, and intent\n    session_id = await store.create_session()\n    surface_id = await store.register_surface(session_id, \"canvas\")\n    utterance_id = await store.create_utterance(session_id, \"test escalation\")\n    topic_id = await store.create_topic(\n        label=\"Test Bead\",\n        topic_type=\"project\",\n        project_slugs=[\"test\"],\n        scope=\"session\",\n        session_id=session_id,\n    )\n\n    # Create intent with bead_ref pointing at our test bead\n    bead_id = \"test-bead-123\"\n    intent_id = await store.create_intent(\n        utterance_id=utterance_id,\n        session_id=session_id,\n        project_slug=\"test\",\n        intent_type=\"escalate\",\n        bead_ref=bead_id,\n        topic_id=topic_id,\n    )\n\n    # Register SSE connection for the active surface\n    conn = broadcaster.register(\n        surface_id=surface_id,\n        session_id=session_id,\n        surface_type=\"canvas\",\n    )\n\n    # Step 2: Create a BeadWatcher with short poll interval\n    watcher = BeadWatcher(\n        store=store,\n        router=router,\n        bf_bin=\"bf\",  # Will be mocked\n        bf_workspace=str(scratch_beads_workspace.parent),  # Parent dir contains .beads\n        check_interval_seconds=1.0,  # Short interval for testing\n    )\n\n    # Set the high-water mark to a past timestamp so the first poll emits events\n    # (otherwise the watcher treats it as a baseline run and emits nothing)\n    watcher._close_highwater = 0.0\n\n    # Step 3: Mock the bf CLI to return a closed bead\n    closed_at = datetime.now().isoformat() + \"Z\"\n    mock_bead_record = {\n        \"id\": bead_id,\n        \"title\": \"Test Bead\",\n        \"description\": \"Test result\",\n        \"status\": \"closed\",\n        \"closed_at\": closed_at,\n        \"labels\": [\"urgency=normal\"],\n    }\n\n    async def mock_run_bf_list_closed():\n        \"\"\"Mock bf list --status closed --json to return our test bead.\"\"\"\n        return [mock_bead_record]\n\n    # Patch the _run_bf_list_closed method\n    watcher._run_bf_list_closed = mock_run_bf_list_closed\n\n    # Step 4: Run one tick of the watcher\n    await watcher._check_for_events()\n\n    # Step 5: Assert results row was written\n    results = await store.get_results_for_intent(intent_id)\n    assert len(results) == 1\n    result = results[0]\n    assert result[\"intent_id\"] == intent_id\n    assert result[\"topic_id\"] == topic_id\n    assert result[\"session_id\"] == session_id\n    assert \"Test result\" in result[\"summary\"]\n    assert json.loads(result[\"data\"])[\"bead_id\"] == bead_id\n\n    # Step 6: Assert intent was resolved\n    intent = await store.get_intent(intent_id)\n    assert intent[\"status\"] == \"resolved\"\n\n    # Step 7: Assert SSE event was fired on the active surface\n    # The event should be in the connection's queue\n    event = await asyncio.wait_for(conn.queue.get(), timeout=1.0)\n    assert event.event_type == \"result_created\"\n    assert event.data[\"result_id\"] == result[\"id\"]\n    assert event.data[\"session_id\"] == session_id","new_string":"@pytest.mark.asyncio\nasync def test_bead_close_creates_result_and_sse(\n    store: SessionStore,\n    broadcaster: SSEBroadcaster,\n    router: SurfaceRouter,\n    scratch_beads_workspace: Path,\n):\n    \"\"\"\n    Integration test: closing a bead writes a result, resolves intent, and fires SSE.\n\n    This test verifies the complete close->result path:\n    1. Create an intent with bead_ref pointing at a test bead\n    2. Simulate bf CLI returning the closed bead\n    3. Run one watcher tick\n    4. Assert:\n       - Results row written to DB\n       - Intent status updated to resolved\n       - SSE result_created event fired on active surface\n    \"\"\"\n    # Step 1: Set up session, surface, utterance, topic, and intent\n    session_id = await store.create_session()\n    surface_id = await store.register_surface(session_id, \"canvas\")\n    utterance_id = await store.create_utterance(session_id, \"test escalation\")\n    topic_id = await store.create_topic(\n        label=\"Test Bead\",\n        topic_type=\"project\",\n        project_slugs=[\"test\"],\n        scope=\"session\",\n        session_id=session_id,\n    )\n\n    # Create intent with bead_ref pointing at our test bead\n    bead_id = \"test-bead-123\"\n    intent_id = await store.create_intent(\n        utterance_id=utterance_id,\n        session_id=session_id,\n        project_slug=\"test\",\n        intent_type=\"escalate\",\n        bead_ref=bead_id,\n        topic_id=topic_id,\n    )\n\n    # Register SSE connection for the active surface\n    conn = broadcaster.register(\n        surface_id=surface_id,\n        session_id=session_id,\n        surface_type=\"canvas\",\n    )\n\n    # Step 2: Create a BeadWatcher with short poll interval\n    watcher = BeadWatcher(\n        store=store,\n        router=router,\n        bf_bin=\"bf\",  # Will be mocked\n        bf_workspace=str(scratch_beads_workspace.parent),  # Parent dir contains .beads\n        check_interval_seconds=1.0,  # Short interval for testing\n    )\n\n    # Set the high-water mark to a past timestamp so the first poll emits events\n    # (otherwise the watcher treats it as a baseline run and emits nothing)\n    watcher._close_highwater = 0.0\n\n    # Step 3: Mock the bf CLI to return a closed bead\n    closed_at = datetime.now().isoformat() + \"Z\"\n    mock_bead_record = {\n        \"id\": bead_id,\n        \"title\": \"Test Bead\",\n        \"description\": \"Test result\",\n        \"status\": \"closed\",\n        \"closed_at\": closed_at,\n        \"labels\": [\"urgency=normal\"],\n    }\n\n    async def mock_run_bf_list_closed():\n        \"\"\"Mock bf list --status closed --json to return our test bead.\"\"\"\n        return [mock_bead_record]\n\n    # Patch the _run_bf_list_closed method\n    watcher._run_bf_list_closed = mock_run_bf_list_closed\n\n    # Patch the global broadcaster to use our test broadcaster\n    # The watcher uses broadcast_result() which calls get_broadcaster()\n    import src.sse.broadcaster\n    with patch.object(src.sse.broadcaster, 'get_broadcaster', return_value=broadcaster):\n\n        # Step 4: Run one tick of the watcher\n        await watcher._check_for_events()\n\n        # Step 5: Assert results row was written\n        results = await store.get_results_for_intent(intent_id)\n        assert len(results) == 1\n        result = results[0]\n        assert result[\"intent_id\"] == intent_id\n        assert result[\"topic_id\"] == topic_id\n        assert result[\"session_id\"] == session_id\n        assert \"Test result\" in result[\"summary\"]\n        assert json.loads(result[\"data\"])[\"bead_id\"] == bead_id\n\n        # Step 6: Assert intent was resolved\n        intent = await store.get_intent(intent_id)\n        assert intent[\"status\"] == \"resolved\"\n\n        # Step 7: Assert SSE event was fired on the active surface\n        # The event should be in the connection's queue\n        event = await asyncio.wait_for(conn.queue.get(), timeout=1.0)\n        assert event.event_type == \"result_created\"\n        assert event.data[\"result_id\"] == result[\"id\"]\n        assert event.data[\"session_id\"] == session_id"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":0,"output_tokens":0},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"d45d9330-fc42-4658-9fe9-17f6dd1cbe1b","uuid":"6e903397-fc81-4cfb-b08b-0020204ff3a4","timestamp":"2026-07-23T02:38:34.694Z"}

Create intent with bead_ref pointing at test bead\n2. Close bead via bf CLI (simulated via CLI output mocking)\n3. Assert results row written, intent resolved, SSE event fires within one poll interval\n\nThis test uses a scratch .beads workspace and never touches the real production workspace.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport asyncio\nimport json\nimport tempfile\nfrom datetime import datetime\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom unittest.mock import AsyncMock, MagicMock, patch\n\nimport pytest\n\nfrom src.session.store import SessionStore\nfrom src.sse.broadcaster import SSEBroadcaster, SSEEvent\nfrom src.surface.router import SurfaceRouter\nfrom src.watcher.daemon import BeadWatcher\n\n\n@pytest.fixture\nasync def store(tmp_path: Path) -> SessionStore:\n    \"\"\"An isolated SessionStore on a tmp DB.\"\"\"\n    db_path = tmp_path / \"session.db\"\n    s = SessionStore(db_path)\n    await s.initialize()\n    yield s\n    await s.close()\n\n\n@pytest.fixture\nasync def broadcaster() -> SSEBroadcaster:\n    \"\"\"A fresh SSEBroadcaster per test.\"\"\"\n    b = SSEBroadcaster()\n    await b.start()\n    yield b\n    await b.stop()\n\n\n@pytest.fixture\ndef router(store: SessionStore) -> SurfaceRouter:\n    \"\"\"A SurfaceRouter for testing.\"\"\"\n    return SurfaceRouter(store)\n\n\n@pytest.fixture\ndef scratch_beads_workspace(tmp_path: Path) -> Path:\n    \"\"\"A scratch .beads workspace for testing.\"\"\"\n    workspace = tmp_path / \".beads\"\n    workspace.mkdir()\n    return workspace\n\n\n@pytest.mark.asyncio\nasync def test_bead_close_creates_result_and_sse(\n    store: SessionStore,\n    broadcaster: SSEBroadcaster,\n    router: SurfaceRouter,\n    scratch_beads_workspace: Path,\n):\n    \"\"\"\n    Integration test: closing a bead writes a result, resolves intent, and fires SSE.\n\n    This test verifies the complete close->result path:\n    1. Create an intent with bead_ref pointing at a test bead\n    2. Simulate bf CLI returning the closed bead\n    3. Run one watcher tick\n    4. Assert:\n       - Results row written to DB\n       - Intent status updated to resolved\n       - SSE result_created event fired on active surface\n    \"\"\"\n    # Step 1: Set up session, surface, utterance, topic, and intent\n    session_id = await store.create_session()\n    surface_id = await store.register_surface(session_id, \"canvas\")\n    utterance_id = await store.create_utterance(session_id, \"test escalation\")\n    topic_id = await store.create_topic(\n        label=\"Test Bead\",\n        topic_type=\"project\",\n        project_slugs=[\"test\"],\n        scope=\"session\",\n        session_id=session_id,\n    )\n\n    # Create intent with bead_ref pointing at our test bead\n    bead_id = \"test-bead-123\"\n    intent_id = await store.create_intent(\n        utterance_id=utterance_id,\n        session_id=session_id,\n        project_slug=\"test\",\n        intent_type=\"escalate\",\n        bead_ref=bead_id,\n        topic_id=topic_id,\n    )\n\n    # Register SSE connection for the active surface\n    conn = broadcaster.register(\n        surface_id=surface_id,\n        session_id=session_id,\n        surface_type=\"canvas\",\n    )\n\n    # Step 2: Create a BeadWatcher with short poll interval\n    watcher = BeadWatcher(\n        store=store,\n        router=router,\n        bf_bin=\"bf\",  # Will be mocked\n        bf_workspace=str(scratch_beads_workspace.parent),  # Parent dir contains .beads\n        check_interval_seconds=1.0,  # Short interval for testing\n    )\n\n    # Set the high-water mark to a past timestamp so the first poll emits events\n    # (otherwise the watcher treats it as a baseline run and emits nothing)\n    watcher._close_highwater = 0.0\n\n    # Step 3: Mock the bf CLI to return a closed bead\n    closed_at = datetime.now().isoformat() + \"Z\"\n    mock_bead_record = {\n        \"id\": bead_id,\n        \"title\": \"Test Bead\",\n        \"description\": \"Test result\",\n        \"status\": \"closed\",\n        \"closed_at\": closed_at,\n        \"labels\": [\"urgency=normal\"],\n    }\n\n    async def mock_run_bf_list_closed():\n        \"\"\"Mock bf list --status closed --json to return our test bead.\"\"\"\n        return [mock_bead_record]\n\n    # Patch the _run_bf_list_closed method\n    watcher._run_bf_list_closed = mock_run_bf_list_closed\n\n    # Step 4: Run one tick of the watcher\n    await watcher._check_for_events()\n\n    # Step 5: Assert results row was written\n    results = await store.get_results_for_intent(intent_id)\n    assert len(results) == 1\n    result = results[0]\n    assert result[\"intent_id\"] == intent_id\n    assert result[\"topic_id\"] == topic_id\n    assert result[\"session_id\"] == session_id\n    assert \"Test result\" in result[\"summary\"]\n    assert json.loads(result[\"data\"])[\"bead_id\"] == bead_id\n\n    # Step 6: Assert intent was resolved\n    intent = await store.get_intent(intent_id)\n    assert intent[\"status\"] == \"resolved\"\n\n    # Step 7: Assert SSE event was fired on the active surface\n    # The event should be in the connection's queue\n    event = await asyncio.wait_for(conn.queue.get(), timeout=1.0)\n    assert event.event_type == \"result_created\"\n    assert event.data[\"result_id\"] == result[\"id\"]\n    assert event.data[\"session_id\"] == session_id\n\n\n@pytest.mark.asyncio\nasync def test_bead_close_with_no_intent_is_skipped(\n    store: SessionStore,\n    router: SurfaceRouter,\n    scratch_beads_workspace: Path,\n):\n    \"\"\"\n    Test that closing a bead with no matching intent is skipped gracefully.\n\n    Most bf beads are not escalate-tracked, so the watcher should skip them\n    without error.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Create a watcher\n    watcher = BeadWatcher(\n        store=store,\n        router=router,\n        bf_bin=\"bf\",\n        bf_workspace=str(scratch_beads_workspace.parent),\n        check_interval_seconds=1.0,\n    )\n\n    # Mock bf to return a bead with no matching intent\n    bead_id = \"untracked-bead-456\"\n    closed_at = datetime.now().isoformat() + \"Z\"\n    mock_bead_record = {\n        \"id\": bead_id,\n        \"title\": \"Untracked Bead\",\n        \"status\": \"closed\",\n        \"closed_at\": closed_at,\n        \"labels\": [],\n    }\n\n    async def mock_run_bf_list_closed():\n        return [mock_bead_record]\n\n    watcher._run_bf_list_closed = mock_run_bf_list_closed\n\n    # Run one tick - should not raise any error\n    await watcher._check_for_events()\n\n    # No results should exist\n    all_results = await store.get_all_results()\n    assert len(all_results) == 0\n\n\n@pytest.mark.asyncio\nasync def test_bead_close_with_missing_topic_id_is_skipped(\n    store: SessionStore,\n    router: SurfaceRouter,\n    scratch_beads_workspace: Path,\n):\n    \"\"\"\n    Test that closing a bead whose intent has no topic_id is skipped.\n\n    This handles edge cases where intent state is incomplete.\n    \"\"\"\n    session_id = await store.create_session()\n    utterance_id = await store.create_utterance(session_id, \"test\")\n\n    # Mock bf to return the closed bead\n    closed_at = datetime.now().isoformat() + \"Z\"\n    mock_bead_record = {\n        \"id\": bead_id,\n        \"title\": \"Orphan Bead\",\n        \"description\": \"No topic\",\n        \"status\": \"closed\",\n        \"closed_at\": closed_at,\n        \"labels\": [],\n    }\n\n    async def mock_run_bf_list_closed():\n        return [mock_bead_record]\n\n    watcher._run_bf_list_closed = mock_run_bf_list_closed\n\n    # Run one tick - should log warning but not crash\n    await watcher._check_for_events()\n\n    # Intent should still be pending (not resolved)\n    intent = await store.get_intent(intent_id)\n    assert intent[\"status\"] == \"pending\"\n\n    # No result should exist\n    results = await store.get_results_for_intent(intent_id)\n    assert len(results) == 0\n\n\n@pytest.mark.asyncio\nasync def test_highwater_mark_prevents_duplicate_delivery(\n    store: SessionStore,\n    broadcaster: SSEBroadcaster,\n    router: SurfaceRouter,\n    scratch_beads_workspace: Path,\n):\n    \"\"\"\n    Test that the close-timestamp high-water mark prevents re-delivery of\n    already-closed beads on restart.\n\n    First tick after start should baseline the mark and emit nothing.\n    Only beads closed AFTER the mark should be delivered.\n    \"\"\"\n    session_id = await store.create_session()\n    surface_id = await store.register_surface(session_id, \"canvas\")\n    utterance_id = await store.create_utterance(session_id, \"test\")\n    topic_id = await store.create_topic(\n        label=\"Test Topic\",\n        topic_type=\"project\",\n        project_slugs=[\"test\"],\n        scope=\"session\",\n        session_id=session_id,\n    )\n\n    bead_id = \"baseline-bead-999\"\n    intent_id = await store.create_intent(\n        utterance_id=utterance_id,\n        session_id=session_id,\n        project_slug=\"test\",\n        intent_type=\"escalate\",\n        bead_ref=bead_id,\n    )\n\n    conn = broadcaster.register(\n        surface_id=surface_id,\n        session_id=session_id,\n        surface_type=\"canvas\",\n    )\n\n    watcher = BeadWatcher(\n        store=store,\n        router=router,\n        bf_bin=\"bf\",\n        bf_workspace=str(scratch_beads_workspace.parent),\n        check_interval_seconds=1.0,\n    )\n\n    # First tick: return existing closed bead\n    # Should baseline the mark and emit nothing\n    closed_at = datetime.now().isoformat() + \"Z\"\n    mock_existing_bead = {\n        \"id\": bead_id,\n        \"title\": \"Existing Closed Bead\",\n        \"description\": \"Already closed\",\n        \"status\": \"closed\",\n        \"closed_at\": closed_at,\n        \"labels\": [\"urgency=normal\"],\n    }\n\n    async def mock_run_bf_list_closed():\n        return [mock_existing_bead]\n\n    watcher._run_bf_list_closed = mock_run_bf_list_closed\n\n    #
